input.background: Exercises 16.2 and 16.4 considered a problem in which seven of eight patients expressed a preference for medical procedure A compared to medical procedure B. A Normal approximation test was precluded because of the small sample size.
input.question: What test statistic is used, and what is the observed number of successes in the sample?

output.answer: An exact binomial test is used because of the small sample size. Under $H_0, X \sim \mathrm{~b}(8,0.5)$. The observed number of success in the sample is $x=$ 7.
